name: Python docs — dict.update method signature
goal:
Open https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict.update (the Python 3 stdlib reference for dict.update) and extract the function signature line shown for dict.update.

Return ONLY a JSON object with EXACTLY this key:
  - "signature": the signature line as a string. It MUST contain 'update(' (the method name and opening paren) since that's the canonical Python method signature format.

Example correct output:
  {"signature": "update([other])"}

The signature is shown in monospace font right next to the dict.update heading in the Python docs. Complete with EXACTLY this JSON object as your final result.
